'''Century flowers''' were a rare [[Plant/Legends|vegetal]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] on the far side of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] from [[Thustra/Legends|Thustra]]. Specimens were nurtured by [[King/Legends|King]] [[Alaric (Sephi)|Alaric]] in [[Alaric's garden|his garden]] on Thustra for a [[Standard century/Legends|century]]. Those [[Flower/Legends|flowers]] were sensitive and delicate, and he carefully protected them from weeds. Alaric used his gardening as a metaphor to explain to [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] why he believed Thustra should secede from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="JY" />

==Behind the scenes==
The centennial flower of King Alaric appeared in the [[2004]] comic ''[[Jedi: Yoda]]'', the fifth and final issue in the [[Star Wars: Jedi (one-shots)|''Star Wars: Jedi'']] series of comics, written by [[Jeremy Barlow]] and illustrated by [[HOON]].<ref name="JY" /> In HOON's former gallery of concept art, that flower was referred to as a "Sephiorchid," hinting that it may be a kind of [[Orchid/Legends|orchid]].<ref name="HOON">{{WebCite|url=http://www.messs.cc/gallery/SWCONCEPT/sephiorchid|text=Star Wars Jedi Yoda Concept: Sephiorchid|work=[http://www.messs.cc Mess Studio Art Gallery]|archivedate=20080401033114}}</ref>
